enrichment.short_description: A no-code, click-to-edit website builder with built-in e-commerce, blogging, and analytics for entrepreneurs and creatives.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Strikingly is a no-code website builder with click-to-edit design, a built-in store, blog, and analytics for creatives and entrepreneurs.

features.title: Click-to-edit visual editor
features.description: Click any element on the page to edit it directly and publish instantly, with no code or design experience required.

features.title: Simple Store
features.description: Sell products with built-in e-commerce, connecting to PayPal or Stripe, plus coupons, shipping, and digital goods.

features.title: Simple Blog
features.description: Publish blog posts that each get their own dedicated page, built directly into the site editor.

features.title: Built-in analytics
features.description: Every site includes easy-to-read visitor analytics with no separate integration needed.

features.title: Sign-up, contact, and membership tools
features.description: Add sign-up forms, contact forms, live chat, newsletters, and gated membership content.

features.title: Social feed embedding
features.description: Display Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram feeds directly on a site.

features.title: Free HTTPS
features.description: All published sites get HTTPS automatically at no extra cost.

features.title: AI Site Builder and AI Logo Maker
features.description: AI-assisted tools are available for generating a starting site and a logo.

pros: No coding or design skills required to publish a working site
Built-in e-commerce and blogging without needing extra plugins
Free HTTPS and analytics included on every site
Available in 18 interface languages and has a dedicated iPhone app
cons: Custom domain connection requires upgrading from the free plan
Mobile editing app is iPhone-only, with no Android app mentioned on the site
Detailed paid-plan pricing isn't published in a simple static table on the pricing page

faqs.question: Does Strikingly support e-commerce?
faqs.answer: Yes, through the built-in Simple Store, which connects to PayPal or Stripe and supports coupons, shipping, and digital goods.

faqs.question: Do I need coding skills to use Strikingly?
faqs.answer: No, Strikingly is a no-code, click-to-edit builder aimed at people with no design or development background.

faqs.question: Is there a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Strikingly offers an iPhone app for building and editing sites on the go.

faqs.question: Does Strikingly include a blog?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Simple Blog feature is built into the editor and gives each post its own page.

use_cases.title: One-page portfolios and landing pages
use_cases.description: Creatives and freelancers use Strikingly's templates to launch a mobile-optimized site in under 30 minutes.

use_cases.title: Small online stores
use_cases.description: Businesses sell a single product or a full catalog using the built-in Simple Store with PayPal/Stripe checkout.

use_cases.title: Blogging and audience building
use_cases.description: Writers publish updates through the Simple Blog and grow an audience with newsletters and sign-up forms.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Strikingly?
paragraphs: Strikingly is a website builder built around a click-to-edit interface: users click directly on any element of a template to change it, without touching code or a separate design tool.
Beyond the site editor, it bundles a store (Simple Store), a blog (Simple Blog), sign-up and contact forms, membership gating, and free built-in analytics and HTTPS on every published site.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Strikingly is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at entrepreneurs, creatives, and small businesses who want a simple, mobile-optimized site or one-page store without hiring a developer or designer.

enrichment.short_description: A free, wizard-style website builder with built-in hosting, SEO tools, and an online store option, with custom domains as a paid add-on.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SITE123 is a free website builder with built-in hosting and SEO tools; custom domains and stores are available on paid plans from $10.8/mo.

features.title: Free-forever core plan
features.description: The base plan is free indefinitely, with no credit card required to sign up, and includes hosting and the editor.

features.title: Guided website editor
features.description: A structured, wizard-style editor where users upload content into pre-built layouts rather than a traditional drag-and-drop canvas.

features.title: Built-in SEO tools
features.description: SEO settings are built into the editor to help pages be indexed and found in search engines.

features.title: Free hosting on CDN infrastructure
features.description: Sites are hosted on globally distributed CDN servers to reduce load time for visitors.

features.title: Online store
features.description: Users can create an online store and sell products globally directly from their site.

features.title: Custom domain connection
features.description: A custom domain can be connected on a paid plan starting as low as $10.8/month on an annual, prepaid plan.

features.title: Royalty-free image bank
features.description: An integrated image bank with royalty-free photos is available for use in site design.

features.title: 24/7 live chat support
features.description: Live chat support is available at any time to help with building and managing the site.

pros: Genuinely free core plan with no credit card required and no time limit
Built-in SEO tools and CDN-based free hosting included from the start
24/7 live chat support plus a 14-day money-back guarantee on paid plans
No limit on the number of websites per account
cons: Custom domain and full e-commerce features require a paid upgrade
Editor is more template/wizard-driven than a fully flexible drag-and-drop builder
Sites cannot be downloaded — SITE123 is a closed, proprietary hosting platform

faqs.question: Is SITE123 really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the product is offered at no cost, including hosting and the full editor, with no payment or trial commitment required.

faqs.question: How much does a custom domain cost?
faqs.answer: Connecting a custom domain starts as low as $10.8/month if paid upfront on an annual plan.

faqs.question: How many websites can I create?
faqs.answer: There is no limit — a single account can create and manage multiple websites.

faqs.question: Do I need design skills to use SITE123?
faqs.answer: No, users upload content and pick from ready-made layouts rather than designing from scratch.

use_cases.title: First business website
use_cases.description: Small businesses use the guided editor to get a site online in under an hour without design help.

use_cases.title: Basic online store
use_cases.description: Sellers set up products and start selling online using the built-in store tools.

use_cases.title: Personal or portfolio sites
use_cases.description: Non-technical individuals build a free personal site or portfolio using pre-made layouts.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SITE123?
paragraphs: SITE123 is a website builder centered on a guided, wizard-style editor: rather than free-form drag-and-drop, users select a website type and upload their own content into ready-made layouts.
The core plan is free indefinitely with built-in hosting, SEO tools, and support for an online store, while a custom domain and extra storage/bandwidth are paid upgrades.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SITE123 is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at non-technical users and small businesses who want the fastest possible path to a working website without design or coding skills.

enrichment.short_description: A German website and online store builder offering both a simple no-code editor and a code-customizable Creator editor.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Jimdo is a German website and online store builder with a free plan, a simple editor, and a code-customizable Creator editor for more control.

features.title: Two editors: Website Builder and Creator
features.description: A simple no-code website/store builder plus a Creator editor with coding features for custom navigation, stores, and design elements.

features.title: Online Store Builder
features.description: A dedicated builder for setting up an online store, separate from the general website builder.

features.title: Online booking
features.description: Customers can take appointments directly through the website via built-in booking tools.

features.title: Smart forms
features.description: Contact forms can be created for any occasion directly in the editor.

features.title: Business listings (Jimdo Local)
features.description: Manages business listings on Google, Facebook, and other platforms from one place.

features.title: Social selling integration
features.description: Products and content can be promoted and sold directly on Facebook and Instagram.

features.title: Warning-proof legal texts
features.description: Generates privacy policy and terms text intended to meet legal disclosure requirements.

pros: Long operating history — in business since 2007 with over 32 million Jimdo websites created
Offers both a simple editor and a code-customizable Creator editor for teams that want more control
Built-in tools for bookings, forms, and legal pages beyond basic site design
Free plan available, with the option to upgrade at any time
cons: No customer service phone hotline — support is via help center, live chat, or email only
Paying month-to-month costs more than committing to a 12- or 24-month term
Choosing between the Website Builder and Creator editors upfront matters, since their feature sets differ

faqs.question: Can I start free and upgrade later?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can start with a free website and upgrade to a paid plan at any time from the site dashboard.

faqs.question: Can I connect my own domain?
faqs.answer: Yes, premium plans support transferring an existing domain to Jimdo or connecting it while keeping it registered elsewhere.

faqs.question: How do I pay for a plan?
faqs.answer: You can pay monthly, or save money by paying upfront for a 12- or 24-month term.

faqs.question: Can I cancel my contract?
faqs.answer: Consumers get a 14-day right of withdrawal where a statutory right applies; this does not apply to business accounts.

use_cases.title: Local business websites
use_cases.description: Restaurants, coaches, and practices use Jimdo to combine a simple site with online booking and business listings.

use_cases.title: Small online stores
use_cases.description: Sellers use the Online Store Builder together with Facebook/Instagram selling integration.

use_cases.title: Custom-coded sites
use_cases.description: Teams that need more design control use the Creator editor to hand-code navigation and layout.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Jimdo?
paragraphs: Jimdo is a German website and online store builder, in operation since 2007, that offers two distinct editors: a simple, guided Website/Store Builder and a more advanced Creator editor with coding features.
Beyond page design, it includes booking tools, smart forms, business listing management for Google and Facebook, and legal-text generation for privacy policies and terms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Jimdo is for
paragraphs: It suits local businesses and solopreneurs who want a fast, low-maintenance site or store, as well as developers who want the option to hand-code parts of the site through the Creator editor.

enrichment.short_description: A free publishing platform for paid newsletters, podcasts, and video, where writers keep 90% of subscription revenue.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Substack is a free platform for paid newsletters, podcasts, and video where writers keep 90% of subscriber revenue and own their list.

features.title: Free publishing platform
features.description: Writers can start a publication with no upfront platform fee and no contract to sign.

features.title: Built-in paid subscriptions
features.description: Writers set a subscription price and readers can pay directly through Substack; the writer keeps 90% of that revenue.

features.title: Built-in reader network
features.description: More than half of new subscribers come from Substack's built-in network and recommendations rather than outside promotion.

features.title: Multi-format publishing
features.description: Supports written posts as well as podcasts, video, and live video, not just text newsletters.

features.title: Full ownership of list and payments
features.description: Writers retain ownership of their intellectual property, mailing list, and subscriber payments.

features.title: Substack app
features.description: A dedicated app is available for reading and managing publications.

pros: No cost to start publishing or to launch a paid subscription business
Large existing reader network that surfaces new subscribers through recommendations
Writers keep full ownership of their subscriber list, content, and payouts
Supports podcasts and video in addition to written newsletters
cons: Substack takes a 10% cut of paid subscription revenue, on top of separate payment-processing fees
Built primarily around newsletter/post publishing rather than general-purpose website design
Discovery relies partly on Substack's own network, meaning growth is tied to the platform

faqs.question: Is Substack free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's free to start and free to leave, with no upfront platform fee, tiers, or contract.

faqs.question: How does Substack make money?
faqs.answer: It takes 10% of a writer's paid subscription revenue; the remaining 90% goes to the writer.

faqs.question: Can I publish more than text newsletters?
faqs.answer: Yes, Substack supports podcasts, video, and live video alongside written posts.

faqs.question: Do I own my subscriber list?
faqs.answer: Yes, writers retain ownership of their intellectual property, mailing list, and subscriber payments.

use_cases.title: Paid newsletters
use_cases.description: Independent writers and journalists monetize their writing directly through reader subscriptions.

use_cases.title: Paid podcasts
use_cases.description: Podcasters use Substack's subscription tools to charge for exclusive episodes.

use_cases.title: Niche expertise publications
use_cases.description: Finance writers, food writers, and other subject-matter experts build a direct paying audience.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Substack?
paragraphs: Substack is a publishing platform where writers and creators distribute newsletters, podcasts, and video, with built-in tools for charging readers a subscription fee.
It's free to start, and Substack's revenue comes from taking a 10% cut of paid subscription revenue rather than charging a platform subscription fee.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Substack is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at independent writers, journalists, podcasters, and niche experts who want to build a direct, paid relationship with their audience without giving up ownership of their list or content.

enrichment.short_description: A competitor-monitoring and pipeline-intelligence platform that surfaces named contacts in active sales cycles with your competitors.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Letterdrop is a competitor-monitoring platform that surfaces named contacts in live competitor sales cycles for B2B revenue teams.

features.title: Competitor sales-cycle monitoring
features.description: Tracks up to 3-5 named competitors and surfaces the specific contacts actively talking to them, with a confidence score.

features.title: Daily signal delivery
features.description: Delivers signals daily to Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Apollo, Outreach, Clay, or a spreadsheet, with a recommended outreach play attached.

features.title: Fast time-to-value
features.description: First signals arrive within 10 days, with no implementation project required.

features.title: Native CRM and outreach integrations
features.description: Integrates natively with Salesforce, HubSpot, Gong, Fathom, Circleback, Google Sheets, and Zapier.

features.title: MCP integration with ChatGPT and Claude
features.description: Connects via Model Context Protocol so AI assistants can query Letterdrop data and take actions inside the platform.

features.title: SOC 2 compliant data handling
features.description: Customer data is encrypted in transit and at rest, and is not used to train Letterdrop's models.

pros: Surfaces named contacts in live competitor sales cycles, not just anonymous account-level research signals
Delivers signals directly into existing CRM and outreach tools without a new system of record
First signals arrive within 10 days with no implementation project
SOC 2 compliant and does not use customer data to train models
cons: No public self-serve pricing — plans require a demo or sales conversation
Best suited to companies with 50+ employees and an established outbound motion
Stated competitor-signal accuracy is around 30% on average, so not every surfaced contact converts

faqs.question: What does Letterdrop do?
faqs.answer: It identifies contacts who are actively starting sales cycles with your competitors, tracking public signals to surface live conversations before a vendor is chosen.

faqs.question: How is Letterdrop different from 6sense or Bombora?
faqs.answer: 6sense and Bombora track account-level content research; Letterdrop tracks contacts in live conversations forming with specific named competitors.

faqs.question: What does Letterdrop integrate with?
faqs.answer: Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Apollo.io, Outreach, Clay, Gong, Fathom, Circleback, Google Sheets, and Zapier, plus ChatGPT and Claude via MCP.

faqs.question: How accurate is Letterdrop's competitor monitoring?
faqs.answer: It identifies contacts at roughly 30% accuracy on average for active competitor sales cycles, and up to about 1 in 2 in some verticals.

use_cases.title: Intercepting competitor deals
use_cases.description: Sales-led B2B companies use Letterdrop to reach named contacts before a competitor closes the deal.
use_cases.audience: VPs of Sales and RevOps leaders

use_cases.title: Outbound signal enrichment
use_cases.description: Outbound agencies and Clay power-users layer Letterdrop's signals on top of enrichment workflows.

use_cases.title: Founder-led GTM
use_cases.description: Early-stage startups use Letterdrop to activate founders online and generate inbound demos.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Letterdrop?
paragraphs: Letterdrop is a competitor-monitoring and pipeline-intelligence platform for B2B sales teams. It identifies specific contacts who are actively starting sales cycles with a company's chosen competitors, rather than showing account-level research interest.

Signals are delivered daily into Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Apollo, Outreach, Clay, or a spreadsheet, each with a confidence score and a recommended outreach play, and the platform also connects to ChatGPT and Claude via MCP.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Letterdrop is for
paragraphs: It's built for sales-led companies with at least 50 employees and a repeatable outbound motion, including VPs of Sales, demand gen leaders, outbound agencies, and Clay power-users.

enrichment.short_description: An API-first headless CMS with a free plan, official framework SDKs, and built-in SEO and AI content-assist tools.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ButterCMS is an API-first headless CMS with a free tier, SDKs for React/Next.js/Angular/Django/Laravel, and built-in SEO and AI tools.

features.title: Content modeling
features.description: Structure content once and reuse it across websites, ecommerce, and mobile apps.

features.title: Developer tooling
features.description: APIs, SDKs, and webhooks with official integrations for Angular, Django, Laravel, Next.js, and React.

features.title: Workflow and collaboration
features.description: User roles and approval flows for streamlining content publishing across a team.

features.title: Multisite and multi-language support
features.description: Manage multiple brands, regions, and languages from a single CMS instance.

features.title: Built-in SEO fields
features.description: SEO metadata and fast-loading HTML delivery are built into content pages.

features.title: AI Assistant
features.description: AI-powered content blocks and suggestions to speed up content creation.

features.title: Media and asset management
features.description: Centralized media library with drag-and-drop uploads and edits.

pros: Free plan with no credit card required, plus a 14-day free trial on every paid plan
Official SDKs/integrations for React, Vue, Next.js, Angular, Django, and Laravel
Published, self-serve pricing through the Professional tier
Includes an AI Assistant and built-in SEO metadata on every plan
cons: Usage is tightly metered — API calls, bandwidth, pages, blog posts, and assets all have plan-specific caps
Multi-locale support isn't available until the Professional plan
Enterprise features (custom limits, dedicated support) require contacting sales for custom pricing

faqs.question: Do I need a credit card for the Free plan?
faqs.answer: No, the Free plan gives full access to core features with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What's included in the Free plan?
faqs.answer: Unlimited users and components, 50K API calls/month, 100GB bandwidth/month, up to 5 pages, 50 blog posts, 50 collections, and 500 assets.

faqs.question: Does ButterCMS offer a free trial on paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, every paid plan includes a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: Which frameworks does ButterCMS officially support?
faqs.answer: Angular, Django, Laravel, Next.js, and React have official integrations and quickstart guides.

use_cases.title: Adding a CMS layer to an app
use_cases.description: Developers add ButterCMS as a headless content layer to an existing product using its APIs and SDKs.

use_cases.title: Marketing-led publishing
use_cases.description: Marketers publish and update landing pages and blog content without filing developer tickets.

use_cases.title: Multi-brand content management
use_cases.description: Teams manage content across multiple sites, brands, or languages from one CMS.

features: Custom API calls, bandwidth, pages, collections, assets, roles, and locales

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ButterCMS?
paragraphs: ButterCMS is an API-first headless CMS designed to let marketers publish content quickly while developers integrate it into React, Vue, Next.js, Angular, Django, or Laravel projects using official SDKs.
It includes workflow tools (roles and approval flows), multisite/multi-language support, built-in SEO fields, and an AI Assistant for content creation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ButterCMS is for
paragraphs: It suits developers who want a hosted, API-driven CMS without building one from scratch, and marketers who need to publish and update content without waiting on engineering.

enrichment.short_description: A composable digital experience platform combining a headless CMS with AI-driven personalization, analytics, and automation.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Contentful is a composable digital experience platform combining a headless CMS with AI-driven personalization and analytics.

features.title: Composable content platform
features.description: Delivers content across web, apps, and other channels from a single source.

features.title: Personalization
features.description: Tools for creating and running personalized, tailored experiences for different audience segments.

features.title: AI Actions
features.description: Automates and streamlines content workflows using AI.

features.title: Studio
features.description: Visually assemble digital experiences at scale.

features.title: Palmata
features.description: A newer Contentful product focused on understanding and improving how an organization is represented in AI discovery/search.

features.title: Marketplace
features.description: Apps and integrations for extending the platform's capabilities.

features.title: Free plan quotas
features.description: The Free plan includes 100K API calls/month and 50GB asset bandwidth/month, with no overages allowed.

pros: Broader platform than a basic CMS, adding personalization, analytics, and AI-driven content automation
Published customer case studies show measurable results (e.g. faster release cycles, higher conversion rates)
Free plan available with a meaningful included quota (100K API calls, 50GB bandwidth/month)
Marketplace of apps and integrations for extending the platform
cons: Paid tier (Lite/Enterprise) pricing isn't published publicly and requires contacting sales
Free plan is hard-capped with no overages allowed on API calls or bandwidth
Broader platform scope (personalization, analytics, AI Actions) adds more surface area than a simple headless CMS

faqs.question: Does Contentful have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a Free plan is available and includes a Starter Space with published usage limits.

faqs.question: Is pricing public for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Only the Free plan has published self-serve limits; Lite and Enterprise plans require contacting sales.

faqs.question: What is Palmata?
faqs.answer: Palmata is Contentful's newer product for understanding and improving how an organization is represented in AI discovery and search.

faqs.question: Does Contentful support personalization?
faqs.answer: Yes, Contentful Personalization lets teams create tailored experiences and run experimentation.

use_cases.title: Composable digital experience platform
use_cases.description: Enterprises use Contentful to unify content delivery across web, personalization, and analytics.

use_cases.title: Headless content delivery
use_cases.description: Developers use Contentful's APIs to power custom front-ends.

use_cases.title: Personalization and experimentation
use_cases.description: Growth and marketing teams run audience segmentation and experiments on content.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Contentful?
paragraphs: Contentful is a composable digital experience platform that combines a headless CMS with personalization, AI-driven content automation (AI Actions), and analytics tools like Studio.
It also offers Palmata, a newer product focused on how organizations are represented in AI-powered discovery and search.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Contentful is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at enterprise and mid-market teams that need to manage content across many channels and want personalization and analytics built into the same platform, alongside developers building headless front-ends.

enrichment.short_description: A headless CMS with a visual, component-based editor, an API-first architecture, and a free Starter plan.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Storyblok is a headless CMS with a visual, component-based editor, API-first delivery, and a free Starter plan for small projects.

features.title: Visual editor with Global Components
features.description: Component-based content blocks that developers build once and marketers reuse and rearrange visually.

features.title: API-first delivery
features.description: GraphQL API, REST API, and Management APIs, plus an MCP Server for AI agent access.

features.title: Multi-locale content
features.description: Locale support included from the free Starter plan, with more locales available on higher tiers.

features.title: AI credits
features.description: Included AI credits (25k on the Starter plan) power AI-assisted content features.

features.title: Seat-based collaboration
features.description: Plans include a set number of user seats, with additional seats available for a per-seat fee.

features.title: ISO 27001 certified security
features.description: Certified security practices with up to 99.99% uptime SLA on enterprise tiers.

features.title: Native integrations
features.description: Includes integrations such as Shopify on the Growth plan and above.

pros: Free Starter plan plus a 45-day free trial of the Growth Plus plan for new projects
Transparent, published self-serve pricing through the Growth and Growth Plus tiers
API-first architecture with a visual editor, suited to both developers and marketers
ISO 27001 certified with high uptime SLAs on higher tiers
cons: Team size, locales, traffic, and API calls are all metered, with extra cost on every tier
Enterprise features (custom roles, SSO, SCIM, 99.9% SLA) require a custom Premium/Elite quote
Free Starter plan is limited to 1 space and 2 locales

faqs.question: Does Storyblok have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Starter plan is free and includes 1 space, 1 user seat, and 100GB traffic per month.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 45-day free trial of the Growth Plus plan is offered before committing to a paid plan.

faqs.question: When was Storyblok founded?
faqs.answer: Storyblok was started in 2017 in Austria by founders Dominik and Alexander.

faqs.question: Does Storyblok support AI or agent workflows?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers an MCP Server and includes AI credits for AI-assisted content features.

use_cases.title: Visual content editing
use_cases.description: Marketing teams use the visual editor and Global Components to build and rearrange pages without code.

use_cases.title: Headless site development
use_cases.description: Developers build front-ends with Next.js, Nuxt, or Astro against Storyblok's APIs.

use_cases.title: Multi-brand enterprise content
use_cases.description: Enterprises manage content across multiple brands, regions, and locales.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Storyblok?
paragraphs: Storyblok is a headless CMS founded in 2017 in Austria, built around a visual editor and reusable component blocks ('Global Components') on top of an API-first architecture (GraphQL, REST, and Management APIs).
It offers a free Starter plan, a self-serve Growth and Growth Plus tier with published pricing, and custom Premium/Elite tiers for enterprise needs like SSO and custom SLAs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Storyblok is for
paragraphs: It's built for teams where both developers and marketers need to work in the same CMS — developers building reusable components, marketers assembling pages visually without code.

enrichment.short_description: A headless CMS and page builder with AI translation and an MCP integration letting ChatGPT or Claude update pages via prompt.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Prismic is a headless CMS and page builder with reusable slices, AI translation, and an MCP integration for ChatGPT and Claude.

features.title: Visual Page Builder
features.description: A headless CMS where pages are assembled from reusable, brand-approved sections called slices.

features.title: Slice Machine
features.description: A local developer tool for coding and customizing slices within a normal development workflow.

features.title: AI translation
features.description: Creates and manages multilingual content while keeping each locale aligned to a master page structure.

features.title: AI landing page builder
features.description: A separately priced add-on that uses AI to scale creation of on-brand landing pages.

features.title: MCP integration with ChatGPT and Claude
features.description: AI assistants can query and edit pages across the site directly through a single prompt.

features.title: Built-in SEO/GEO schema support
features.description: Pages include schema support aimed at visibility in AI search and Google Search.

features.title: Migration API
features.description: Included even on the Free plan for moving content into or out of Prismic.

pros: Free plan includes 4M API calls/month and unlimited documents, types, and assets
Self-serve pricing is fully published from Free through the Platinum tier
Founded in 2013 with 10,000+ websites built on the platform
Native MCP integration lets ChatGPT and Claude make prompt-driven edits across pages
cons: User seats are limited on lower tiers — 1 user on Free, 3 on Starter
The AI landing page builder is a separate paid add-on, not included in base plans
Enterprise features like SSO and a dedicated Customer Success Manager require a custom quote

faqs.question: Is Prismic free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes 1 user, 4 million API calls/month, and 100GB of CDN bandwidth/month at no cost.

faqs.question: How is Prismic priced?
faqs.answer: Per repository, billed annually, ranging from Free up to Platinum at $675/month, with Enterprise available on request.

faqs.question: Where is Prismic based?
faqs.answer: Prismic's headquarters is in Paris, France.

faqs.question: Does Prismic support AI-assisted editing?
faqs.answer: Yes, including AI translation and prompt-driven page updates through its MCP integration with ChatGPT and Claude.

use_cases.title: SEO and ABM landing pages
use_cases.description: Marketing teams build landing pages for SEO and account-based marketing at scale using slices.

use_cases.title: Component-based site development
use_cases.description: Developers build reusable page sections with Next.js or Nuxt using Slice Machine.

use_cases.title: Multi-client site management
use_cases.description: Agencies manage multiple client websites from a single platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Prismic?
paragraphs: Prismic, founded in 2013 in Paris, is a headless CMS and page builder built around reusable content sections called slices, developed locally with a tool called Slice Machine.
It has since added AI features including AI translation, an AI landing page add-on, and an MCP integration that lets ChatGPT and Claude query and edit pages directly through a prompt.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Prismic is for
paragraphs: It's used by marketing teams building SEO and ABM landing pages, developers building reusable page sections, and agencies managing multiple client sites on one platform.

enrichment.short_description: A headless CMS with a GraphQL API, structured text editor, built-in localization, and a free-forever plan.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: DatoCMS is a headless CMS with a GraphQL API, structured text editor, built-in localization, and a free-forever plan.

features.title: GraphQL Content Delivery API
features.description: Content is delivered through a GraphQL-based Content Delivery API alongside a REST Content Management API.

features.title: Structured Text editor
features.description: A block-based rich text editor for structured, reusable content.

features.title: Built-in localization
features.description: Multi-locale content management is included, with 5 locales on both the Free and Professional plans.

features.title: Modular content modeling
features.description: Content is built from reusable, component-based models for dynamic page building.

features.title: Visual Editing
features.description: Content can be edited directly on the page rather than hunting through records.

features.title: Plugin ecosystem
features.description: Over 200 plugins are available to expand and customize the CMS.

features.title: Cache Tags
features.description: Fine-grained cache invalidation for high-performance content delivery.

pros: Free-forever plan with no credit card required, and a no-signup live demo
Transparent published pricing for both Free and Professional tiers, with clear add-on pricing for overages
Large plugin marketplace (200+) and an active Slack developer community (3,000+ members)
Structured Text and modular content modeling suited to complex, multi-format sites
cons: Free plan is capped at 300 records, 10GB traffic/month, and 1 collaborator, with no overages allowed
Higher usage (extra projects, records, bandwidth, API calls) is billed as recurring add-ons on top of the Professional price
Community-based support only on the Free plan; faster email support requires the paid Professional tier

faqs.question: Is DatoCMS free?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a free-forever plan with 2 editors, 300 records, and 100k API calls/month, with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much is the paid plan?
faqs.answer: The Professional plan starts at €149/month billed annually, or €199/month billed monthly.

faqs.question: What happens if I exceed the Free plan's limits?
faqs.answer: The service stops responding as expected, since no overages are allowed on the Free plan.

faqs.question: Does DatoCMS support multiple languages?
faqs.answer: Yes, localization is built in, with 5 locales included on both the Free and Professional plans and additional locales available as an add-on.

use_cases.title: Agency client sites
use_cases.description: Digital agencies build multilingual client sites and get a discount through DatoCMS's Agency Partner Program.

use_cases.title: Modern front-end development
use_cases.description: Developers build with Next.js, Nuxt, Astro, or SvelteKit against DatoCMS's GraphQL API.

use_cases.title: Structured content management
use_cases.description: Content teams manage modular, structured content across a website using the Structured Text editor.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is DatoCMS?
paragraphs: DatoCMS is a headless CMS built around a GraphQL Content Delivery API, a Structured Text editor, and built-in localization, aimed at both developers and content teams.

It offers a free-forever plan with published limits (2 editors, 300 records, 100k API calls/month) and a Professional plan starting at €149/month billed annually, with clearly priced add-ons for extra usage.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who DatoCMS is for
paragraphs: It's used by developers building with modern frameworks like Next.js, Nuxt, and Astro, as well as digital agencies and content teams managing structured, multilingual content.
